parameters.content: Runs this inventory check automatically every day at the scheduled time.

## Collect Product & Sales Data
This step gathers all products from the store, checks how much stock is available, looks at sales from the last 30 days, and combines everything to understand how fast each product is selling.

## Inventory Evaluation, Planning & Decision
This step combines product and sales data, checks stock health based on demand, categorizes products by performance, calculates safety stock and reorder quantity, assigns urgency levels, and finally decides which products need immediate attention and alerts.

## Inventory Record, Summarize & Notify Inventory Status

This step saves the latest inventory results into the system, creates a daily summary of stock health and risk items, and sends a clear report to the team on Slack so everyone stays informed.
Sends instant Slack alerts for products that require urgent action or immediate reorder.

## How it works

This workflow automatically checks your store’s inventory every day and helps you avoid stock issues.

First, it pulls all active products and recent completed orders from WooCommerce. Using sales from the last 30 days, it calculates how fast each product is selling.
Next, it compares current stock with demand to understand how healthy your inventory is.

Each product is then classified into clear categories like Top Performer, Steady, At Risk, or Consider Discontinue. Based on this, the system calculates how much safety stock is needed, when to reorder, and how many units should be ordered.

Products that need attention are given an urgency level (Normal, High, or Critical).
Important items trigger alerts, while all results are saved for reporting. Finally, a daily inventory summary is sent to Slack so the team can quickly review overall stock health without checking multiple systems.

This process runs automatically and requires no manual effort once set up.


## Setup steps

**1.** Connect your WooCommerce account (products and orders).

**2.** Connect Supabase to store inventory records.

**3.** Connect Slack to receive alerts and daily summaries.

**4.** Set the schedule time for daily checks.

**5.** Review stock thresholds (lead time, safety days) if needed.
